title: A Standardized Culture Medium for Comparative Drug Efficacy Evaluation Across Plasmodium and Babesia Species
author: Pratap Vydyam
license_note: PMC full-text XML — Bio-protocol — CC-BY confirmed in article XML

Giemsa stain solution 1 (three-step fixative) (Fisher Scientific, catalog number:

steps.title: Human packed RBCs
steps.instruction: Human packed RBCs: Human erythrocytes (Group type A+ for optimal growth) obtained from American Red Cross or Interstate Blood Bank-USA, collected in citrate phosphate dextrose (CPD), washed twice with incomplete medium, and stored at 4 °C for a maximum of 4 weeks

steps.title: Parasite species and strains
steps.instruction: Parasite species and strains: a. Plasmodium falciparum 3D7: (MRA-102; BEI Resources) b. Plasmodium falciparum Dd2: (MRA-150; BEI Resources) c. Plasmodium falciparum HB3: (MRA-1227; BEI Resources) d. Babesia duncani WA1: (NR-12311; BEI Resources) e. Babesia divergens Rouen87: (NR-52008; BEI Resources) f. Babesia MO1: (NR-50441; BEI Resources) Reagents

steps.title: Heat-inactivated fetal bovine serum (FBS) (Gibco,...
steps.instruction: Heat-inactivated fetal bovine serum (FBS) (Gibco, catalog number: 16140071); store at -80 °C

steps.title: Antifolate drugs
steps.instruction: Antifolate drugs: Dihydrotriazines (51 compounds) and biguanides (28 compounds), sourced from Jacobus Pharmaceuticals, purity ≥95% (confirmed by reverse-phase HPLC) Solutions

steps.title: RPMI complete culture medium Reagent Final concentration RPMI-1640 99
steps.instruction: RPMI complete culture medium Reagent Final concentration RPMI-1640 99.3% Albumax-II 0.5% L-Glutamine 2 mM Hypoxanthine 50 mg/L NaHCO 3 0.225% HEPES 25 mM Gentamicin 10 mg/mL

steps.title: DFS20 complete culture medium Reagent Final...
steps.instruction: DFS20 complete culture medium Reagent Final concentration DMEM-F12 77% Heat-inactivated FBS 20% 50× HT Media Supplement Hybri-Max TM 2% L-Glutamine 1% Penicillin/Streptomycin 1% Gentamicin 1%

steps.title: SYBR Green-I lysis buffer Reagent Final concentration Saponin 0
steps.instruction: SYBR Green-I lysis buffer Reagent Final concentration Saponin 0.008% Triton X-100 0.08% Tris-HCl, pH 7.5 20 mM EDTA 5 mM SYBR Green-I 0.01% Note: Prepare the lysis buffer in distilled water and add SYBR Green-I to a concentration of 0.01%. Protect SYBR Green-I from light to prevent photobleaching.

steps.title: Centrifuge A+ packed RBCs at 1,000...
steps.instruction: Centrifuge A+ packed RBCs at 1,000 rcf (acceleration 9 and break

steps.title: for 15 min at 4 °C...
steps.instruction: for 15 min at 4 °C to separate the remaining buffy coat and RBCs.

steps.title: Wash RBCs three times with 10...
steps.instruction: Wash RBCs three times with 10 mL of cold 1× PBS (centrifuge at 700 rcf for 5 min each wash).

steps.title: Resuspend RBCs in incomplete RPMI-1640 medium...
steps.instruction: Resuspend RBCs in incomplete RPMI-1640 medium to achieve 50% hematocrit (e.g., 10 mL of packed RBCs + 10 mL of DFS

steps.title: and store at 4 °C
steps.instruction: and store at 4 °C. B. Determining initial parasitemia

steps.title: Prepare thin blood smears from stock cultures of Babesia spp
steps.instruction: Prepare thin blood smears from stock cultures of Babesia spp. and Plasmodium spp. strains, fix with a fixative solution (10 s), stain with Giemsa (Solution I and II) for 30 s each, wash off excess stain, and air-dry the slide.

steps.title: Count at least 3,000 RBCs under...
steps.instruction: Count at least 3,000 RBCs under a 100× oil immersion objective across 10 different fields on the slide to calculate the percentage of parasitemia.

steps.title: Dilute cultures with fresh RBCs in...
steps.instruction: Dilute cultures with fresh RBCs in complete DFS20 and/or RPMI-1640 medium to achieve 0.2% parasitemia at 5% hematocrit for assay setup.

steps.title: Calculate in vitro parasitemia count as instructed in [33,35,42]
steps.instruction: Calculate in vitro parasitemia count as instructed in [33,35,42]. C. Maintain stock cultures

steps.title: Hematocrit
steps.instruction: Hematocrit: Hematocrit (HCT) refers to the percentage of RBCs in a given volume of blood, representing the packed cell volume. Fresh human RBCs are stored at 50% hematocrit for laboratory use and maintained at 4 °C for up to two weeks. Routine asexual blood-stage cultures are maintained at 2%–5% hematocrit, depending on growth requirements.

steps.title: Percent parasitemia
steps.instruction: Percent parasitemia: Parasitemia refers to the total number of all stages of (mixed stages) parasite-infected RBCs within a total red blood cell population in the in vitro cultures and is commonly expressed as a percentage. Routine maintenance cultures are kept at 1%–3% parasitemia to prevent overgrowth and nutrient limitation. Cultures that reach >5% parasitemia are diluted with fresh RBCs and complete medium to maintain healthy exponential growth and prevent synchronization loss.

steps.title: Babesia duncani (WA-1), B. divergens (Rouen87) , and B. MO1
steps.instruction: Babesia duncani (WA-1), B. divergens (Rouen87) , and B. MO1 : Culture parasites in DFS20 medium at 5% hematocrit (A + human RBCs) at 37 °C under a gas mixture of 2% O 2 , 5% CO 2 , and 93% N 2 . Change medium daily and determine parasitemia from Giemsa-stained thin blood smears by light microscopy.

steps.title: strains
steps.instruction: strains: Culture parasites in RPMI-1640 complete culture medium at 3%–5% hematocrit (A + human RBCs) at 37 °C under a gas mixture of 4% O 2 , 5% CO 2 , and 91% N 2 . Change medium daily and monitor parasitemia as above.

steps.title: Subculture parasites every two generations and...
steps.instruction: Subculture parasites every two generations and maintain parasitemia between 1% and 4% to ensure optimal growth conditions. For Plasmodium strains, synchronize cultures every 5–8 generations. Critical:

steps.title: Ensure that stock cultures are healthy before starting the assay
steps.instruction: Ensure that stock cultures are healthy before starting the assay. Monitor parasite growth to confirm exponential growth. B. duncani parasitemia typically doubles every 24 h, while that of P. falciparum increases by 4-fold every 48 h (one generation time is 48 h).

steps.title: Include all three antibiotics when culturing...
steps.instruction: Include all three antibiotics when culturing multiple parasite species in parallel to minimize contamination risk, although gentamicin alone is sufficient for cultures. Part II. Preparation of drug solutions Time required: 1–2 h. A. Prepare stock solutions

steps.title: Dissolve compounds in 100% DMSO to...
steps.instruction: Dissolve compounds in 100% DMSO to make 10 mM stock solutions. Note: Ensure complete dissolution by vortexing for 10–15 s. Store at -20 °C for long-term storage. Troubleshooting: If precipitation occurs, warm to 50 °C and vortex again. Use an ultrasonic bath if necessary. B. Prepare working dilutions

steps.title: Perform serial 2-fold dilutions in complete...
steps.instruction: Perform serial 2-fold dilutions in complete DFS20 medium to create a 4-step or 12-step concentration range (e.g., 100, 50, 10, and 5 nM to 0.049 nM final concentration in assay wells).

steps.title: Use a 96-well clear flat-bottom plate...
steps.instruction: Use a 96-well clear flat-bottom plate for dilutions to facilitate multichannel pipetting.

steps.title: Ensure final DMSO concentration in assay...
steps.instruction: Ensure final DMSO concentration in assay wells does not exceed 0.5% (v/v).

steps.title: Prepare working drug dilutions at 2× concentrations. Note
steps.instruction: Prepare working drug dilutions at 2× concentrations. Note: Prepare fresh dilutions on the day of the experiment to avoid degradation or precipitation. Part III. Set up the growth inhibition assay Time required: 1–2 h for setup. A. Prepare assay plates in DFS20 medium

steps.title: In a 96-well flat-clear bottom plate...
steps.instruction: In a 96-well flat-clear bottom plate (columns 1–12, rows A–H), add 100 μL of DFS20 medium to all wells except those designated for the initial drug dilutions (e.g., first column of triplicate wells for each drug, such as A1, B1, C1) (see Figure 2 ). Figure

steps.title: 96-well assay plate showing a schematic...
steps.instruction: 96-well assay plate showing a schematic representation of serial dilutions of drugs, including positive and negative controls. Each well accommodates 200 μL of culture volume ( Plasmodium or Babesia parasites) with drugs at various concentrations, prepared by 2-fold serial dilutions. Gradient color indicates the concentration differences in the 96-well flat clear-bottom plate.

steps.title: Add 200 μL of each drug...
steps.instruction: Add 200 μL of each drug dilution (2× working concentration, e.g., 200 nM for a 100 nM final working concentration) to the first column of test wells in triplicate (e.g., rows A–F for two different drugs; see Figure 3 ). Figure

steps.title: Diagram of the SYBR Green I–based...
steps.instruction: Diagram of the SYBR Green I–based high-throughput screening (HTS) assay for assessing drug efficacy against the in vitro asexual life cycle stages of Plasmodium and Babesia parasites in human red blood cells (hRBCs). (A) Preparation of a 96-well clear flat-bottom drug plate with 2-fold serial dilutions in 100 μL of DFS20 medium. (B) Addition of 100 μL of hRBC-infected parasite culture to the drug plate (96-well flat clear bottom plate) using a multichannel pipette. (C) Incubation of the parasite–drug mixture in a 96-well clear flat-bottom plate at 37 °C in a chamber with 4% O 2 , 5% CO 2 , 91% N 2 , and humidification for 72 h. (D) Transfer of incubated cultures to a black-bottom 96-well plate, followed by the addition of an equal volume of SYBR Green I lysis buffer to the parasite culture. (E) Measurement of SYBR Green I fluorescence to quantify parasite DNA content, with excitation at 480 nm and emission at 520 nm. (F) Export of fluorescence data for analysis, including generation of heat maps and determination of IC 50 with standard deviation values using Microsoft Excel and GraphPad Prism.

steps.title: Perform a 2-fold serial dilution by...
steps.instruction: Perform a 2-fold serial dilution by transferring 100 μL from the first wells (2× drug concentration) to the subsequent wells in each row, continuing until the 12th well. Discard the final 100 μL from the 12th well to maintain consistent volumes.

steps.title: Add 100 μL of DFS20 medium with 0
steps.instruction: Add 100 μL of DFS20 medium with 0.5% DMSO to no-drug control wells (maximum parasite growth; H1–H3).

steps.title: Add 100 μL of DFS20 medium...
steps.instruction: Add 100 μL of DFS20 medium with 2 μM WR99210 (for Babesia spp.) or 10× IC 50 concentration of chloroquine (for Plasmodium spp.) to positive control wells (for 100% parasite inhibition; wells H4–H6). Note: Ensure a uniform cell suspension by gently pipetting before dispensing. From this point forward, all parasite cultures and drug treatments are maintained in DFS20 medium. B. Add parasite cultures to the assay plate

steps.title: Add 100 μL of parasite culture (0
steps.instruction: Add 100 μL of parasite culture (0.4% parasitemia, 10% hematocrit in DFS20 medium, 2× concentration) to all test wells with 2× drug concentrations. The final volume in each well should be 200 μL, with both drug and parasite at 1× concentrations.

steps.title: Add 100 μL of uninfected RBCs...
steps.instruction: Add 100 μL of uninfected RBCs (10% hematocrit in DFS20 medium) to background control wells (H10–H12). Critical: Plasmodium cultures grown in RPMI-1640 need to be washed twice with DFS20 (700 rcf, 5 min, at room temperature). Use a multichannel pipette for consistency. Pipette slowly to avoid bubbles. C. Incubate plates

steps.title: Cover the plate with a low-evaporation...
steps.instruction: Cover the plate with a low-evaporation lid to allow gas exchange.

steps.title: Incubate at 37 °C in a...
steps.instruction: Incubate at 37 °C in a humidified chamber with 4% O 2 , 5% CO 2 , and 91% N 2 for 72 h. D. Measurement of parasite growth Time required: 2–3 h.

steps.title: Prepare SYBR Green-I lysis buffer according to the provided recipe
steps.instruction: Prepare SYBR Green-I lysis buffer according to the provided recipe.

steps.title: After 72 h of incubation, remove...
steps.instruction: After 72 h of incubation, remove the 96-well clear flat-bottom drug and parasite mix plate from the incubator.

steps.title: Gently resuspend the culture in each...
steps.instruction: Gently resuspend the culture in each well by pipetting up and down 5 times to ensure homogeneity.

steps.title: Transfer 100 μL of each culture...
steps.instruction: Transfer 100 μL of each culture to a new black flat-bottom 96-well plate containing 100 μL of SYBR Green-I lysis buffer per well.

steps.title: Mix gently by pipetting and incubate...
steps.instruction: Mix gently by pipetting and incubate the plate at 37 °C in the dark (covered with aluminum foil) for 15 min to allow complete lysis and DNA staining.

steps.title: Read fluorescence using a BioTek Synergy...
steps.instruction: Read fluorescence using a BioTek Synergy Mx Microplate Reader (or equivalent, e.g., Spectra Mx) with excitation at 480 nm and emission at 540 nm. (These wavelengths were routinely used and well-established to detect the SYBR Green base assessment.) Critical: Protect SYBR Green-I from light to prevent photobleaching. Some protocols may require a 1-h incubation for complete lysis; adjust as per your experimental requirements. Troubleshooting: If fluorescence is low, ensure thorough mixing for complete RBC lysis. E. Data analysis Time required: 1–2 h.

steps.title: After completing the fluorescence reading, export...
steps.instruction: After completing the fluorescence reading, export the total fluorescence values from the assay to an Excel spreadsheet.

steps.title: Each assay plate includes positive control wells (e
steps.instruction: Each assay plate includes positive control wells (e.g., treated with WR99210 or chloroquine, CQ) and background control wells (uninfected RBCs).

steps.title: Calculate the average fluorescence (AFU) of...
steps.instruction: Calculate the average fluorescence (AFU) of the uninfected RBC wells (background control). Subtract this background fluorescence value from all test wells, negative control wells (0.1% DMSO), and positive control wells to normalize the data.

steps.title: For each test well, calculate the...
steps.instruction: For each test well, calculate the percent growth and inhibition using the following formulas: % G r o w t h = ( F l u o r e s c e n c e o f t e s t w e l l - F l u o r e s c e n c e o f p o s i t i v e c o n t r o l ) ( F l u o r e s c e n c e o f n e g a t i v e c o n t r o l - F l u o r e s c e n c e o f p o s i t i v e c o n t r o l ) × 100 % I n h i b i t i o n = 100 - % G r o w t h

steps.title: Export the % inhibition values of...
steps.instruction: Export the % inhibition values of each drug concentration in the group format in GraphPad Prism and adjust the color format. Generate the heat maps.

steps.title: Plot percent inhibition (y-axis) against log-transformed...
steps.instruction: Plot percent inhibition (y-axis) against log-transformed drug concentrations (x-axis) using GraphPad Prism.

steps.title: Fit a sigmoidal dose-response curve (variable...
steps.instruction: Fit a sigmoidal dose-response curve (variable slope) to calculate IC 50 values with derived SD from biological triplicate experiments

steps.title: Perform the analysis using data from...
steps.instruction: Perform the analysis using data from three independent experiments, each with technical triplicates. Critical:

steps.title: Negative control (0
steps.instruction: Negative control (0.1% DMSO) represents 0% inhibition (maximum parasite growth).

steps.title: Positive control (WR99210 or CQ-treated) represents...
steps.instruction: Positive control (WR99210 or CQ-treated) represents 100% inhibition (no parasite growth).

steps.title: Uninfected RBC wells serve as the...
steps.instruction: Uninfected RBC wells serve as the background control for fluorescence subtraction. Troubleshooting: If IC 50 curves appear irregular, verify pipetting errors or insufficient drug solubility. Remove or adjust obvious outliers from the dataset as needed.

description: Validation of protocol This protocol (or parts of it) has been used and validated in the following research article: Vydyam et al. [33]. In vitro efficacy of next-generation dihydrotriazines and biguanides against babesiosis and malaria parasites. Antimicrob Agents Chemother (Figure 4).
